name: The Revenge
description: A protagonist pursues retaliation for injustice, often sacrificing morality or peace in the process.

nodes.label: Original State
nodes.definition: The protagonist exists in a state of normalcy or contentment. The audience sees what the character has — relationships, honor, peace — so that its loss will matter.
entry_conditions: Audience sees what the character values and stands to lose
exit_conditions: A grievous wrong is committed against the character or those they love
typical_variants: Happy family life
Honorable position in society
Hard-won peace or retirement
failure_modes: Original state is too brief or vague — the audience doesn't feel the loss
Character has nothing to lose, so the inciting wrong lacks weight

nodes.label: Inciting Wrong
nodes.definition: A devastating injustice is committed against the protagonist — murder, betrayal, violation, or destruction of what they loved. The wrong is severe enough to justify the pursuit that follows.
exit_conditions: The protagonist survives and the desire for revenge ignites
typical_variants: Murder of a loved one
Betrayal by a trusted figure
Unjust imprisonment or exile
Public humiliation and destruction of reputation
failure_modes: Wrong is too mild to justify the scale of revenge
Wrong is committed off-screen and lacks emotional impact
The antagonist's motive is unclear or absent

nodes.label: Vow of Revenge
nodes.definition: The protagonist commits to retaliating against those responsible. The vow reorganizes their entire life around the pursuit of vengeance.
exit_conditions: The protagonist begins actively pursuing revenge
typical_variants: Spoken oath witnessed by others
Silent internal resolve
Ritualistic commitment (burying the dead, marking the body)
The vow is the character's only reason to keep living
failure_modes: Vow is made too casually — no sense of the weight
Character has no credible path to achieving revenge, yet pursues it without addressing this gap

nodes.label: Preparation
nodes.definition: The protagonist builds the capability to execute revenge — training, gathering allies, acquiring resources, or positioning themselves near the target.
entry_conditions: The protagonist lacks the means to act immediately
exit_conditions: The protagonist is ready to begin executing the plan
typical_variants: Years of training or self-improvement
Building a new identity to infiltrate
Recruiting allies with shared grievances
Accumulating wealth or weapons
failure_modes: Preparation is skipped — the protagonist is immediately capable
Preparation takes so long it delays momentum without adding depth
signals_in_text: Transformation of the character's appearance or skills

nodes.label: Pursuit and Escalation
nodes.definition: The protagonist methodically pursues the targets, removing obstacles and agents of the antagonist. Each step escalates the conflict and the moral cost.
entry_conditions: The protagonist begins acting against the targets
exit_conditions: The protagonist is close to the final target but has paid a significant moral or personal cost
typical_variants: Eliminating the antagonist's lieutenants one by one
Dismantling the antagonist's power structure
Cat-and-mouse with the target
Each kill or confrontation extracts a moral toll
failure_modes: Pursuit is a series of easy victories with no cost
No moral deterioration — the protagonist remains perfectly righteous
The target never fights back or adapts
signals_in_text: The line between justice and cruelty blurring

nodes.label: Moral Crossroads
nodes.definition: The protagonist confronts the full cost of the revenge path — what they have become, who they have hurt, or what they have sacrificed. A choice crystallizes: continue or relent.
entry_conditions: The pursuit has exacted a visible toll
A moment of clarity or confrontation forces self-examination
exit_conditions: The protagonist chooses to proceed with the final act or to abandon revenge
typical_variants: An innocent is harmed or threatened by the protagonist's actions
A loved one confronts the protagonist about what they've become
The protagonist sees a mirror of themselves in the antagonist
An opportunity for a different life appears
failure_modes: Crossroads is skipped — the protagonist never questions the path
The moral cost is too theoretical — no concrete harm is shown
signals_in_text: Confrontation with an ally or innocent

nodes.label: Final Reckoning
nodes.definition: The protagonist confronts the primary antagonist in the decisive moment. The encounter resolves the revenge but also determines the protagonist's ultimate fate — redeemed, destroyed, or hollowed out.
entry_conditions: The moral crossroads has been passed
exit_conditions: The antagonist is defeated, pardoned, or escapes; the protagonist's fate is sealed
typical_variants: Kill the antagonist and accept the consequences
Spare the antagonist at the last moment
Both are destroyed
The antagonist is defeated but the protagonist gains nothing
failure_modes: Reckoning is anticlimactic after the buildup
The antagonist is too weak to be a worthy opponent
No connection between the moral crossroads and the final choice
signals_in_text: The final blow or the withheld blow

nodes.label: Aftermath
nodes.definition: The revenge is complete (or abandoned). The protagonist must live with the result — the emptiness of achieved vengeance, the peace of mercy, or the ruin of obsession.
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established — stable but transformed
typical_variants: Hollow victory — the protagonist has what they wanted but has lost themselves
Peace through mercy — the protagonist finds freedom by letting go
Death — the protagonist's destruction completes the cycle
Ambiguous ending — the cost and gain are left for the audience to weigh
failure_modes: Aftermath is skipped — the story ends at the kill
Revenge is presented as purely satisfying with no cost
The moral question raised at the crossroads is never answered

edges.label: Wrong Committed
edges.preconditions: Original state is established; the antagonist acts
edges.effects_on_stakes: Everything the protagonist valued is destroyed or taken
edges.effects_on_character: The protagonist is broken and then remade around a single purpose
edges.common_alternatives: Wrong is sudden and brutal; wrong is a slow betrayal that culminates in loss
edges.anti_patterns: Wrong is too mild to justify the revenge that follows

edges.label: Swears Revenge
edges.preconditions: The wrong is suffered; the protagonist survives
edges.effects_on_stakes: The protagonist's life is now defined entirely by the pursuit
edges.effects_on_character: Identity narrows to a single purpose; former self begins to die
edges.common_alternatives: Vow is immediate and passionate; vow forms slowly during recovery
edges.anti_patterns: Vow is made without real conviction or is easily abandoned

edges.label: Builds Capability
edges.preconditions: The vow is made; the protagonist lacks the current means
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each step toward readiness closes off the possibility of a normal life
edges.effects_on_character: The character transforms — harder, more skilled, more isolated
edges.common_alternatives: Preparation is a montage; preparation is a long infiltration
edges.anti_patterns: Protagonist is immediately capable with no need to prepare

edges.label: Begins the Hunt
edges.preconditions: Preparation is complete; the protagonist moves against the targets
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each action escalates the conflict and the danger to the protagonist
edges.effects_on_character: The moral line between justice and cruelty begins to blur
edges.common_alternatives: Hunt is systematic; hunt is reactive as opportunities arise
edges.anti_patterns: Targets are eliminated without resistance or moral complication

edges.label: Confronts What They've Become
edges.preconditions: The pursuit has exacted a visible toll; someone or something forces reflection
edges.effects_on_stakes: The protagonist's soul is now at stake alongside the external mission
edges.effects_on_character: Self-knowledge arrives — the character sees what revenge has made them
edges.common_alternatives: An ally holds up the mirror; an innocent victim forces the question; the protagonist sees themselves in the enemy
edges.anti_patterns: No moral reckoning ever occurs — the protagonist is never questioned

edges.label: Faces the Enemy
edges.preconditions: The moral crossroads is passed; the protagonist reaches the antagonist
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum — the outcome determines both external justice and the protagonist's moral fate
edges.effects_on_character: The character must act on whatever they decided at the crossroads
edges.common_alternatives: The confrontation is physical; the confrontation is psychological; the confrontation is both
edges.anti_patterns: The final confrontation doesn't reflect the moral question raised at the crossroads

edges.label: Lives With the Result
edges.preconditions: The final reckoning is resolved; the protagonist must face what comes next
edges.effects_on_stakes: External stakes dissolve; internal stakes become permanent
edges.effects_on_character: The character must integrate the entire revenge journey into a new identity — or be consumed by it
edges.common_alternatives: Hollow peace; genuine peace through mercy; death as final cost; ambiguity
edges.anti_patterns: Aftermath is purely triumphant with no reflection on cost
